mainText:
Good morning. Here's your Breeze communications briefing for Wednesday, April 8th.

Breeze is earning clean, positive local television coverage in four markets this morning with no negative press or operational issues anywhere on the radar. Columbus, Wilmington, Jacksonville, and Myrtle Beach all have local stations running stories on Breeze's active promotional fare sale, with one-way fares starting as low as 45 dollars and the sale window closing tomorrow, April 9th, for travel through mid-May. This coverage lands at a particularly favorable moment given that legacy carriers are dominating aviation headlines today for raising bag fees, and Breeze's promotional pricing offers a natural contrast angle that marketing could activate quickly. The simultaneous coverage across four distinct markets signals that the local outreach strategy is working. No operational incidents, no social media concerns, and no analyst commentary to flag in the last 24 hours.

Now for industry and competitor news.

Axios and C.B.S. News reported Tuesday that Delta Air Lines raised its first checked bag fee by ten dollars to 45 dollars, effective for all tickets purchased starting today, becoming the third major carrier in two weeks to hike fees after United moved on April 2nd and JetBlue raised fees in late March. With all three major carriers now at 45 dollars or more for a first bag, Breeze has a real-time opening to remind travelers of our fee approach, and the marketing team should decide today whether to activate a rapid-response message before the news cycle moves on.

C.N.B.C. reported Tuesday that jet fuel supply concerns are intensifying as Strait of Hormuz closures continue to restrict delivery, with jet fuel now sitting at 4.88 dollars per gallon — a 132 percent increase since late February — and United's C.E.O. estimating the Iran conflict has already added 400 million dollars to the airline's operating costs. Breeze's A220 fleet burns roughly 25 percent less fuel than older narrowbody aircraft, making our cost structure meaningfully more resilient than legacy carriers right now and a strong media talking point worth flagging before any upcoming C.E.O. interviews.

Skift reported Tuesday that despite the fuel surge, U.S. airline passenger demand remains resilient, particularly for leisure travel, though analysts warn that sustained fuel prices at current levels could erase industry profits heading into summer. For Breeze's revenue and network teams, strong demand supports continued route expansion, but margin assumptions for the third quarter should be stress-tested against a scenario where fuel stays elevated.

Southwest Airlines announced Tuesday that starting April 20th, passengers will be limited to one portable lithium charger per flight, with power banks banned from overhead bins and in-seat charging ports, following recent International Civil Aviation Organization guidance on lithium battery fire risk. Breeze communications should confirm our portable device policy is current and have a brief holding statement ready in case media begin surveying airlines on their compliance posture.

Delta Air Lines pilots reopened labor contract negotiations this week ahead of their December 2026 amendable date, with their union pushing for a quick deal while Delta's profits remain high and the pay gap with competitors is narrowing, according to Aviation A to Z. A significant Delta pilot wage settlement could reset industry pay benchmarks across U.S. carriers, and Breeze's people and finance teams should factor this into 2027 workforce planning assumptions.

Southwest Airlines completed its inaugural flight Tuesday to Charles M. Schulz Sonoma County Airport in Northern California, launching four nonstop routes including Las Vegas and Denver as the carrier continues its push into smaller, leisure-focused secondary airports. Southwest entering non-hub leisure markets is a trend worth flagging to Breeze network planning, as both carriers are increasingly targeting similar leisure travelers in secondary cities.

That's your briefing. Roger is standing by when you're ready to dive deeper or take action on any of these stories.
